content:
-
You are a scope-aware ReAct agent. On every turn, emit exactly one block of three lines:
  Thought: <atomic> <free-form reasoning at the atomic level>
  Action: {"name": "<tool_name>", "args": {...}}
  Confidence: <integer 0-100>
The current granularity is atomic. Always prefix the Thought with `<atomic>` so the trajectory logger captures the active scope. To stop, emit an Action with name "Finish" and an "answer" arg.

(Prompt template adapted from the LangChain project (Apache License 2.0). See https://github.com/langchain-ai/langchain for the upstream source.)

Problem: Ordering problem in admin.RelatedFieldListFilter and admin.RelatedOnlyFieldListFilter
Description
	
RelatedFieldListFilter doesn't fall back to the ordering defined in Model._meta.ordering. 
Ordering gets set to an empty tuple in ​https://github.com/django/django/blob/2.2.1/django/contrib/admin/filters.py#L196 and unless ordering is defined on the related model's ModelAdmin class it stays an empty tuple. IMHO it should fall back to the ordering defined in the related model's Meta.ordering field.
RelatedOnlyFieldListFilter doesn't order the related model at all, even if ordering is defined on the related model's ModelAdmin class.
That's because the call to field.get_choices ​https://github.com/django/django/blob/2.2.1/django/contrib/admin/filters.py#L422 omits the ordering kwarg entirely.


Continue with the next Thought / Action / Confidence block.

Problem: Ordering problem in admin.RelatedFieldListFilter and admin.RelatedOnlyFieldListFilter
Description
	
RelatedFieldListFilter doesn't fall back to the ordering defined in Model._meta.ordering. 
Ordering gets set to an empty tuple in ​https://github.com/django/django/blob/2.2.1/django/contrib/admin/filters.py#L196 and unless ordering is defined on the related model's ModelAdmin class it stays an empty tuple. IMHO it should fall back to the ordering defined in the related model's Meta.ordering field.
RelatedOnlyFieldListFilter doesn't order the related model at all, even if ordering is defined on the related model's ModelAdmin class.
That's because the call to field.get_choices ​https://github.com/django/django/blob/2.2.1/django/contrib/admin/filters.py#L422 omits the ordering kwarg entirely.

attachment.content:
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- simplify: Review changed code for reuse, quality, and efficiency, then fix any issues found.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- claude-api: Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching. Also handles migrating existing Claude API code between Claude model versions (4.5 → 4.6, 4.6 → 4.7, retired-model replacements).
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks for the Claude API, Anthropic SDK, or Managed Agents; user adds/modifies/tunes a Claude feature (caching, thinking, compaction, tool use, batch, files, citations, memory) or model (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) in a file; questions about prompt caching / cache hit rate in an Anthropic SDK project.
SKIP: file imports `openai`/other-provider SDK, filename like `*-openai.py`/`*-generic.py`, provider-neutral code, general programming/ML.
- add-metric: Interactively register one or more project metrics in meta/metrics/, grounded in project/description.md. Use during project setup or any time a new measurement needs to be tracked across tasks.
- research-internet: Conduct structured internet research and write `research/research_internet.md`.
- setup-remote-machine: Provision, validate, monitor, and tear down remote GPU machines for task execution. Use when a task needs remote machine setup, monitoring, or teardown.
- self-analyze: Analyze a completed Claude Code or Codex session (task execution, brainstorm, project-description, or any other skill invocation) and identify self-corrected mistakes, uncorrected mistakes that affected the result, and human interventions, then propose concrete ARF framework improvements. Prints findings to the chat; does not save reports.
- add-paper: Download a paper, create metadata assets, and register it in the current task.
- human-brainstorm: Run an interactive brainstorming session to review state and choose next actions.
- add-asset-type: Interactively scaffold a new asset type under meta/asset_types/, grounded in project/description.md. Creates a specification.md stub patterned on the built-in asset types. Use during project setup when a custom asset kind is needed; accept "none for now" to skip.
- research-code: Review libraries and prior tasks to write implementation-oriented code research.
- setup-project: One-shot interactive onboarding for a newly forked Glite ARF template. Shows the safety acknowledgement, installs dependencies, runs doctor.py, chains into create-project-description, provisions the paid services the project declared, then invokes the four meta/ sub-skills. Use once per fork, right after cloning.
- compare-literature: Compare task results against published literature and write the comparison output.
- planning: Synthesize research outputs into a task plan with steps, costs, risks, and checks.
- download_paper: Resolve paper identifiers, fetch the PDF and metadata, and prepare inputs for paper asset creation. Use when you need to download a research paper from partial identifiers.
- generate-suggestions: Generate follow-up task suggestions from completed task outputs and project context.
- research-papers: Review downloaded papers and write `research/research_papers.md`.
- self-improvement: Plan and execute a framework change across every related ARF file — specifications, verificators, aggregators, skills, scripts, hooks, tests, materializers, and docs — in a dedicated infrastructure branch and worktree, with style checks and the full test suite. Use whenever anything under `arf/` or generic parts of `meta/` needs to change.
- add-task-type: Interactively add one or more task types to meta/task_types/, grounded in project/description.md. Use during project setup or any time a recurring kind of task needs standardized planning and implementation guidance.
- create-project-description: Guide creation of `project/description.md` and `project/budget.json` for a new or revised ARF project. Use when project goals, scope, or budget need to be defined.
- generate-daily-news: Generate Daily News
- execute-task: Run an ARF task through all required stages and merge the final PR.
- implementation: Execute `plan/plan.md`, produce assets, and verify results.
- create-task: Create a new not-started task folder with task.json and task_description.md. Use when any skill or workflow needs to create a new task.
- check-python-style: Check a Python file against the project Python style guide and report violations. Use after editing Python files or when reviewing code style.
- add-category: Interactively add one or more entries to meta/categories/, grounded in project/description.md. Use during project setup or any time a new cross-cutting tag is needed.
- check-skill: Verify an ARF skill's structure, shared metadata, symlinks, and markdown quality. Use after creating or updating any skill under `arf/skills/`.
- check-markdown-style: Check a Markdown file against the project markdown style guide and report violations. Use after editing `.md` files or when reviewing markdown quality.
- create-dedup-task: Create a deduplication checkpoint task that scans for duplicate papers and overlapping work. Use when the project needs a new dedup checkpoint.
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

aiTitle: Fix ordering in Django admin related field filters

content.thinking:
The user wants me to fix an ordering problem in Django's admin filters. Let me look at the relevant code to understand the issue and implement a fix.

The problem is:
1. `RelatedFieldListFilter` doesn't fall back to `Model._meta.ordering` when no ordering is defined in the related model's `ModelAdmin` class.
2. `RelatedOnlyFieldListFilter` doesn't order at all, even if ordering is defined in the related model's `ModelAdmin` class.

Let me first look at the current state of the filters.py file.

content.content:
1	"""
2	This encapsulates the logic for displaying filters in the Django admin.
3	Filters are specified in models with the "list_filter" option.
4	
5	Each filter subclass knows how to display a filter for a field that passes a
6	certain test -- e.g. being a DateField or ForeignKey.
7	"""
8	
9	import datetime
10	
11	from django.contrib.admin.exceptions import NotRegistered
12	from django.contrib.admin.options import IncorrectLookupParameters
13	from django.contrib.admin.utils import (
14	    build_q_object_from_lookup_parameters,
15	    get_last_value_from_parameters,
16	    get_model_from_relation,
17	    prepare_lookup_value,
18	    reverse_field_path,
19	)
20	from django.core.exceptions import ImproperlyConfigured, ValidationError
21	from django.db import models
22	from django.utils import timezone
23	from django.utils.translation import gettext_lazy as _
24	
25	
26	class ListFilter:
27	    title = None  # Human-readable title to appear in the right sidebar.
28	    template = "admin/filter.html"
29	
30	    def __init__(self, request, params, model, model_admin):
31	        self.request = request
32	        # This dictionary will eventually contain the request's query string
33	        # parameters actually used by this filter.
34	        self.used_parameters = {}
35	        if self.title is None:
36	            raise ImproperlyConfigured(
37	                "The list filter '%s' does not specify a 'title'."
38	                % self.__class__.__name__
39	            )
40	
41	    def has_output(self):
42	        """
43	        Return True if some choices would be output for this filter.
44	        """
45	        raise NotImplementedError(
46	            "subclasses of ListFilter must provide a has_output() method"
47	        )
48	
49	    def choices(self, changelist):
50	        """
51	        Return choices ready to be output in the template.
52	
53	        `changelist` is the ChangeList to be displayed.
54	        """
55	        raise NotImplementedError(
56	            "subclasses of ListFilter must provide a choices() method"
57	        )
58	
59	    def queryset(self, request, queryset):
60	        """
61	        Return the filtered queryset.
62	        """
63	        raise NotImplementedError(
64	            "subclasses of ListFilter must provide a queryset() method"
65	        )
66	
67	    def expected_parameters(self):
68	        """
69	        Return the list of parameter names that are expected from the
70	        request's query string and that will be used by this filter.
71	        """
72	        raise NotImplementedError(
73	            "subclasses of ListFilter must provide an expected_parameters() method"
74	        )
75	
76	
77	class FacetsMixin:
78	    def get_facet_counts(self, pk_attname, filtered_qs):
79	        raise NotImplementedError(
80	            "subclasses of FacetsMixin must provide a get_facet_counts() method."
81	        )
82	
83	    def get_facet_queryset(self, changelist):
84	        filtered_qs = changelist.get_queryset(
85	            self.request, exclude_parameters=self.expected_parameters()
86	        )
87	        return filtered_qs.aggregate(
88	            **self.get_facet_counts(changelist.pk_attname, filtered_qs)
89	        )
90	
91	
92	class SimpleListFilter(FacetsMixin, ListFilter):
93	    # The parameter that should be used in the query string for that filter.
94	    parameter_name = None
95	
96	    def __init__(self, request, params, model, model_admin):
97	        super().__init__(request, params, model, model_admin)
98	        if self.parameter_name is None:
99	            raise ImproperlyConfigured(
100	                "The list filter '%s' does not specify a 'parameter_name'."
101	                % self.__class__.__name__
102	            )
103	        if self.parameter_name in params:
104	            value = params.pop(self.parameter_name)
105	            self.used_parameters[self.parameter_name] = value[-1]
106	        lookup_choices = self.lookups(request, model_admin)
107	        if lookup_choices is None:
108	            lookup_choices = ()
109	        self.lookup_choices = list(lookup_choices)
110	
111	    def has_output(self):
112	        return len(self.lookup_choices) > 0
113	
114	    def value(self):
115	        """
116	        Return the value (in string format) provided in the request's
117	        query string for this filter, if any, or None if the value wasn't
118	        provided.
119	        """
120	        return self.used_parameters.get(self.parameter_name)
121	
122	    def lookups(self, request, model_admin):
123	        """
124	        Must be overridden to return a list of tuples (value, verbose value)
125	        """
126	        raise NotImplementedError(
127	            "The SimpleListFilter.lookups() method must be overridden to "
128	            "return a list of tuples (value, verbose value)."
129	        )
130	
131	    def expected_parameters(self):
132	        return [self.parameter_name]
133	
134	    def get_facet_counts(self, pk_attname, filtered_qs):
135	        original_value = self.used_parameters.get(self.parameter_name)
136	        counts = {}
137	        for i, choice in enumerate(self.lookup_choices):
138	            self.used_parameters[self.parameter_name] = choice[0]
139	            lookup_qs = self.queryset(self.request, filtered_qs)
140	            if lookup_qs is not None:
141	                counts[f"{i}__c"] = models.Count(
142	                    pk_attname,
143	                    filter=models.Q(pk__in=lookup_qs),
144	                )
145	        self.used_parameters[self.parameter_name] = original_value
146	        return counts
147	
148	    def choices(self, changelist):
149	        add_facets = changelist.add_facets
150	        facet_counts = self.get_facet_queryset(changelist) if add_facets else None
151	        yield {
152	            "selected": self.value() is None,
153	            "query_string": changelist.get_query_string(remove=[self.parameter_name]),
154	            "display": _("All"),
155	        }
156	        for i, (lookup, title) in enumerate(self.lookup_choices):
157	            if add_facets:
158	                if (count := facet_counts.get(f"{i}__c", -1)) != -1:
159	                    title = f"{title} ({count})"
160	                else:
161	                    title = f"{title} (-)"
162	            yield {
163	                "selected": self.value() == str(lookup),
164	                "query_string": changelist.get_query_string(
165	                    {self.parameter_name: lookup}
166	                ),
167	                "display": title,
168	            }
169	
170	
171	class FieldListFilter(FacetsMixin, ListFilter):
172	    _field_list_filters = []
173	    _take_priority_index = 0
174	    list_separator = ","
175	
176	    def __init__(self, field, request, params, model, model_admin, field_path):
177	        self.field = field
178	        self.field_path = field_path
179	        self.title = getattr(field, "verbose_name", field_path)
180	        super().__init__(request, params, model, model_admin)
181	        for p in self.expected_parameters():
182	            if p in params:
183	                value = params.pop(p)
184	                self.used_parameters[p] = prepare_lookup_value(
185	                    p, value, self.list_separator
186	                )
187	
188	    def has_output(self):
189	        return True
190	
191	    def queryset(self, request, queryset):
192	        try:
193	            q_object = build_q_object_from_lookup_parameters(self.used_parameters)
194	            return queryset.filter(q_object)
195	        except (ValueError, ValidationError) as e:
196	            # Fields may raise a ValueError or ValidationError when converting
197	            # the parameters to the correct type.
198	            raise IncorrectLookupParameters(e)
199	
200	    @classmethod
201	    def register(cls, test, list_filter_class, take_priority=False):
202	        if take_priority:
203	            # This is to allow overriding the default filters for certain types
204	            # of fields with some custom filters. The first found in the list
205	            # is used in priority.
206	            cls._field_list_filters.insert(
207	                cls._take_priority_index, (test, list_filter_class)
208	            )
209	            cls._take_priority_index += 1
210	        else:
211	            cls._field_list_filters.append((test, list_filter_class))
212	
213	    @classmethod
214	    def create(cls, field, request, params, model, model_admin, field_path):
215	        for test, list_filter_class in cls._field_list_filters:
216	            if test(field):
217	                return list_filter_class(
218	                    field, request, params, model, model_admin, field_path=field_path
219	                )
220	
221	
222	class RelatedFieldListFilter(FieldListFilter):
223	    def __init__(self, field, request, params, model, model_admin, field_path):
224	        other_model = get_model_from_relation(field)
225	        self.lookup_kwarg = "%s__%s__exact" % (field_path, field.target_field.name)
226	        self.lookup_kwarg_isnull = "%s__isnull" % field_path
227	        self.lookup_val = params.get(self.lookup_kwarg)
228	        self.lookup_val_isnull = get_last_value_from_parameters(
229	            params, self.lookup_kwarg_isnull
230	        )
231	        super().__init__(field, request, params, model, model_admin, field_path)
232	        self.lookup_choices = self.field_choices(field, request, model_admin)
233	        if hasattr(field, "verbose_name"):
234	            self.lookup_title = field.verbose_name
235	        else:
236	            self.lookup_title = other_model._meta.verbose_name
237	        self.title = self.lookup_title
238	        self.empty_value_display = model_admin.get_empty_value_display()
239	
240	    @property
241	    def include_empty_choice(self):
242	        """
243	        Return True if a "(None)" choice should be included, which filters
244	        out everything except empty relationships.
245	        """
246	        return self.field.null or (self.field.is_relation and self.field.many_to_many)
247	
248	    def has_output(self):
249	        if self.include_empty_choice:
250	            extra = 1

254	
255	    def expected_parameters(self):
256	        return [self.lookup_kwarg, self.lookup_kwarg_isnull]
257	
258	    def field_admin_ordering(self, field, request, model_admin):
259	        """
260	        Return the model admin's ordering for related field, if provided.
261	        """
262	        try:
263	            related_admin = model_admin.admin_site.get_model_admin(
264	                field.remote_field.model
265	            )
266	        except NotRegistered:
267	            return ()
268	        else:
269	            return related_admin.get_ordering(request)
270	
271	    def field_choices(self, field, request, model_admin):
272	        ordering = self.field_admin_ordering(field, request, model_admin)
273	        return field.get_choices(include_blank=False, ordering=ordering)
274	
275	    def get_facet_counts(self, pk_attname, filtered_qs):
276	        counts = {
277	            f"{pk_val}__c": models.Count(
278	                pk_attname, filter=models.Q(**{self.lookup_kwarg: pk_val})
279	            )
280	            for pk_val, _ in self.lookup_choices
281	        }
282	        if self.include_empty_choice:
283	            counts["__c"] = models.Count(
284	                pk_attname, filter=models.Q(**{self.lookup_kwarg_isnull: True})
285	            )
286	        return counts
287	
288	    def choices(self, changelist):
289	        add_facets = changelist.add_facets
290	        facet_counts = self.get_facet_queryset(changelist) if add_facets else None
291	        yield {
292	            "selected": self.lookup_val is None and not self.lookup_val_isnull,
293	            "query_string": changelist.get_query_string(
294	                remove=[self.lookup_kwarg, self.lookup_kwarg_isnull]
295	            ),
296	            "display": _("All"),
297	        }
298	        count = None
299	        for pk_val, val in self.lookup_choices:
300	            if add_facets:
301	                count = facet_counts[f"{pk_val}__c"]
302	                val = f"{val} ({count})"
303	            yield {
304	                "selected": self.lookup_val is not None
305	                and str(pk_val) in self.lookup_val,
306	                "query_string": changelist.get_query_string(
307	                    {self.lookup_kwarg: pk_val}, [self.lookup_kwarg_isnull]
308	                ),
309	                "display": val,
310	            }
311	        empty_title = self.empty_value_display

316	            yield {

318	                "query_string": changelist.get_query_string(
319	                    {self.lookup_kwarg_isnull: "True"}, [self.lookup_kwarg]
320	                ),
321	                "display": empty_title,
322	            }

content.thinking:
Now I can see the current state of Django's admin filters.py. Looking at the `field_choices` method in `RelatedFieldListFilter`:

```python
def field_choices(self, field, request, model_admin):
    ordering = self.field_admin_ordering(field, request, model_admin)
    return field.get_choices(include_blank=False, ordering=ordering)
```

And `field_admin_ordering`:

```python
def field_admin_ordering(self, field, request, model_admin):


```

This method tries to fetch the related model's admin ordering, catching `NotRegistered` exceptions when the related model isn't registered in admin. The problem is that both when the related model isn't registered and when it is registered but has no ordering defined, the method returns an empty tuple, which means the field choices fall back to the model's default ordering instead of respecting the admin's configuration.

The fix needs to ensure that when `field_admin_ordering` returns empty, it falls back to the related model's `_meta.ordering` as a final fallback. Looking at the Django 2.2.1 code, the issue is that `RelatedOnlyFieldListFilter` isn't properly handling this fallback scenario, so I need to modify `field_admin_ordering` to check the model's default ordering when the admin doesn't define one. Writing the fallback logic...

Looking more carefully at the issue, I see there are actually two separate problems here. The first one about `RelatedFieldListFilter` not falling back to model ordering seems to be what I just addressed. But the second issue with `RelatedOnlyFieldListFilter` is trickier—it appears the newer Django version already has the fix where it calls `field_admin_ordering`, but in the older 2.2.1 version from the bug report, the `field_choices` method doesn't use that ordering at all, it's just pulling raw values from the queryset.

So the remaining issue is that neither filter class falls back to `Model._meta.ordering` when no explicit ordering is set on the ModelAdmin. The fix would be to update `field_admin_ordering` to check for that fallback.

Since this is a concrete Django bug fix task, I need to figure out whether I'm working with actual Django source code or if I should create a patch that could be applied. Let me look at what resources are available for this task.

226	    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(description="t0026 phase-2 A/B/C runtime orchestrator.")
227	    group = parser.add_mutually_exclusive_group(required=True)
228	    group.add_argument("--build-manifest", action="store_true")
229	    group.add_argument("--smoke", action="store_true")
230	    group.add_argument("--preflight", action="store_true")
231	    group.add_argument("--full", action="store_true")
232	    return parser
233

238	    _ = VARIANT_A, VARIANT_B, VARIANT_C
239	    if args.build_manifest:

241	    if args.smoke:

243	    if args.preflight:

245	    if args.full:
246	        return _full_command()
247	    return 1

"""Orchestrator entry: t0026 phase-2 A/B/C runtime; --build-manifest --smoke --preflight --full."""

from __future__ import annotations

import argparse
import json
import sys
import time
from typing import Any

from tasks.t0026_phase2_abc_runtime_n147_for_rq1_rq5.code.anthropic_shim import (
    CostTracker,
    make_model_call,
)
from tasks.t0026_phase2_abc_runtime_n147_for_rq1_rq5.code.instance_loader import (
    Instance,
    load_instances,
    sample_per_subset,
    write_manifest,
)
from tasks.t0026_phase2_abc_runtime_n147_for_rq1_rq5.code.judge import (
    JudgeResult,
    Prediction,
    judge_outcome,
)
from tasks.t0026_phase2_abc_runtime_n147_for_rq1_rq5.code.paths import (
    COST_REESTIMATE_PATH,
    HARD_BUDGET_CAP_USD,
    INSTANCE_MANIFEST_PATH,
    JUDGE_MODEL_PRIMARY,
    MODEL_UNDER_TEST,
    N_FRONTSCI_TARGET,
    N_SWEBENCH_TARGET,
    N_TAUBENCH_TARGET,
    N_TOTAL_TARGET,
    PREFLIGHT_DIR,
    PREFLIGHT_PER_SUBSET,
    SMOKE_TEST_PATH,
    SUBSETS,
    VARIANT_A,
    VARIANT_B,
    VARIANT_C,
    VARIANTS,
)
from tasks.t0026_phase2_abc_runtime_n147_for_rq1_rq5.code.runner import VariantId, run_variant


def _build_manifest_command() -> int:
    instances = load_instances()
    write_manifest(instances=instances, output_path=INSTANCE_MANIFEST_PATH)
    print(f"wrote manifest with {len(instances)} instances to {INSTANCE_MANIFEST_PATH}")
    counts: dict[str, int] = {}
    for inst in instances:
        counts[inst.subset] = counts.get(inst.subset, 0) + 1
    print(f"per-subset counts: {counts}")
    return 0


def _smoke_command() -> int:
    cost_tracker = CostTracker()
    call = make_model_call(model_id=MODEL_UNDER_TEST, cost_tracker=cost_tracker, max_tokens=128)
    prompt = "Reply with the single word: ready"
    response = call(prompt)
    snapshot = cost_tracker.snapshot()
    payload = {
        "model_id": MODEL_UNDER_TEST,
        "prompt": prompt,
        "response": response,
        "cost_snapshot": snapshot,
    }
    SMOKE_TEST_PATH.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
    SMOKE_TEST_PATH.write_text(json.dumps(payload, indent=2) + "\n", encoding="utf-8")
    print(f"smoke test wrote {SMOKE_TEST_PATH}")
    print(f"response: {response[:200]}")
    print(f"cost: ${snapshot['cost_usd']:.6f}, n_calls={snapshot['n_calls']}")
    return 0


def _judge_predictions(
    *,
    predictions: list[Prediction],
    instances_by_id: dict[str, Instance],
    cost_tracker: CostTracker,
) -> dict[str, JudgeResult]:
    results: dict[str, JudgeResult] = {}
    for pred in predictions:
        instance = instances_by_id[pred.instance_id]
        try:
            jr = judge_outcome(
                instance=instance,
                prediction=pred,
                cost_tracker=cost_tracker,
                model_id=JUDGE_MODEL_PRIMARY,
            )
        except Exception as exc:  # noqa: BLE001
            jr = JudgeResult(
                success=False,
                rationale=f"judge call failed: {type(exc).__name__}: {exc!s}"[:300],
                raw_response="",
            )
        results[pred.instance_id] = jr
    return results


def _project_full_run_cost(*, preflight_cost: float) -> float:
    n_runs_preflight = PREFLIGHT_PER_SUBSET * len(SUBSETS) * len(VARIANTS)
    n_judge_calls_preflight = n_runs_preflight
    cost_per_unit = preflight_cost / max(1, n_runs_preflight + n_judge_calls_preflight)
    n_runs_full = N_TOTAL_TARGET * len(VARIANTS)
    n_judge_calls_full = n_runs_full
    n_inter_judge = 30 * len(VARIANTS)
    projected_main = (n_runs_full + n_judge_calls_full) * cost_per_unit
    projected_inter = n_inter_judge * cost_per_unit * (15.0 / 3.0)
    return projected_main + projected_inter


def _preflight_command() -> int:
    instances = load_instances()
    write_manifest(instances=instances, output_path=INSTANCE_MANIFEST_PATH)
    instances_by_id = {inst.instance_id: inst for inst in instances}
    preflight_instances = sample_per_subset(
        instances=instances,
        n_per_subset=PREFLIGHT_PER_SUBSET,
    )
    print(
        f"preflight: {len(preflight_instances)} instances "
        f"(per subset = {PREFLIGHT_PER_SUBSET}); 3 variants -> "
        f"{len(preflight_instances) * len(VARIANTS)} runs + judges"
    )
    cost_tracker = CostTracker()
    model_call = make_model_call(
        model_id=MODEL_UNDER_TEST,
        cost_tracker=cost_tracker,
        max_tokens=2048,
    )
    PREFLIGHT_DIR.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
    started = time.monotonic()
    summaries: dict[str, Any] = {}
    predictions_by_variant: dict[str, list[Prediction]] = {}
    for variant in VARIANTS:
        print(f"--- variant {variant!r} ---")
        variant_id: VariantId = variant  # type: ignore[assignment]
        summary = run_variant(
            variant=variant_id,
            instances=preflight_instances,
            model_call=model_call,
            cost_snapshot=lambda: cost_tracker.snapshot()["cost_usd"],
            output_dir=PREFLIGHT_DIR,
            max_turns=8,
        )
        summaries[variant] = {
            "n_instances": summary.n_instances,
            "elapsed_seconds": summary.elapsed_seconds,
            "predictions": [
                {
                    "instance_id": p.instance_id,
                    "subset": p.subset,
                    "final_answer_excerpt": (p.final_answer or "")[:200],
                    "final_confidence": p.final_confidence,
                    "cost_usd": p.cost_usd,
                }
                for p in summary.predictions
            ],
        }
        predictions_by_variant[variant] = summary.predictions
        cost_so_far = cost_tracker.snapshot()["cost_usd"]
        print(f"  variant {variant} done: cost so far ${cost_so_far:.4f}")
    print("--- judging preflight predictions ---")
    judge_summaries: dict[str, dict[str, Any]] = {}
    for variant, preds in predictions_by_variant.items():
        verdicts = _judge_predictions(
            predictions=preds,
            instances_by_id=instances_by_id,
            cost_tracker=cost_tracker,
        )
        judge_summaries[variant] = {
            iid: {"success": jr.success, "rationale": jr.rationale[:200]}
            for iid, jr in verdicts.items()
        }
    elapsed = time.monotonic() - started
    snapshot = cost_tracker.snapshot()
    preflight_cost = snapshot["cost_usd"]
    projected_full = _project_full_run_cost(preflight_cost=preflight_cost)
    payload: dict[str, Any] = {
        "preflight_n_instances": len(preflight_instances),
        "preflight_n_per_subset": PREFLIGHT_PER_SUBSET,
        "n_variants": len(VARIANTS),
        "preflight_cost_usd": preflight_cost,
        "preflight_n_calls": snapshot["n_calls"],
        "preflight_parse_failures": snapshot["parse_failures"],
        "preflight_elapsed_seconds": elapsed,
        "projected_full_run_cost_usd": projected_full,
        "hard_budget_cap_usd": HARD_BUDGET_CAP_USD,
        "model_under_test": MODEL_UNDER_TEST,
        "judge_model_primary": JUDGE_MODEL_PRIMARY,
        "n_swebench_target": N_SWEBENCH_TARGET,
        "n_taubench_target": N_TAUBENCH_TARGET,
        "n_frontsci_target": N_FRONTSCI_TARGET,
        "summaries_per_variant": summaries,
        "judge_summaries_per_variant": judge_summaries,
        "fits_within_cap": projected_full <= HARD_BUDGET_CAP_USD,
    }
    COST_REESTIMATE_PATH.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
    COST_REESTIMATE_PATH.write_text(json.dumps(payload, indent=2) + "\n", encoding="utf-8")
    print(f"wrote {COST_REESTIMATE_PATH}")
    print(
        f"preflight cost: ${preflight_cost:.4f}, "
        f"projected full run: ${projected_full:.2f}, "
        f"cap: ${HARD_BUDGET_CAP_USD:.2f}"
    )
    if projected_full > HARD_BUDGET_CAP_USD:
        print("PROJECTION EXCEEDS HARD CAP; halting before full sweep.")
        return 2
    return 0


def _full_command() -> int:
    print(
        "--full not implemented in this validation-gate scope. The orchestrator decides go/no-go "
        "based on data/cost_reestimate.json and launches the full sweep separately."
    )
    return 1


def _make_arg_parser() -> argparse.ArgumentParser:
    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(description="t0026 phase-2 A/B/C runtime orchestrator.")
    group = parser.add_mutually_exclusive_group(required=True)
    group.add_argument("--build-manifest", action="store_true")
    group.add_argument("--smoke", action="store_true")
    group.add_argument("--preflight", action="store_true")
    group.add_argument("--full", action="store_true")
    return parser


def main(argv: list[str] | None = None) -> int:
    parser = _make_arg_parser()
    args = parser.parse_args(argv)
    _ = VARIANT_A, VARIANT_B, VARIANT_C
    if args.build_manifest:
        return _build_manifest_command()
    if args.smoke:
        return _smoke_command()
    if args.preflight:
        return _preflight_command()
    if args.full:
        return _full_command()
    return 1


if __name__ == "__main__":
    sys.exit(main())

content.content:
# Python Style (Critical Rules)

Full guide: `arf/styleguide/python_styleguide.md`

* Python 3.12+ syntax (`list[int]` not `List[int]`, `X | None` not `Optional[X]`)
* Line length: 100 characters
* Absolute imports only, never relative
* Keyword arguments for 2+ heterogeneous parameters
* `@dataclass(frozen=True, slots=True)` for all structured data; never return tuples
* Centralize all file paths as `Path` constants in `paths.py`
* Named constants for all magic strings; no hardcoded values
* Strict mypy with explicit type annotations everywhere
* Explicit checks: `if x is None:` not `if not x:`, `if len(lst) == 0:` not `if not lst:`
* `pathlib.Path` always, never `os.path`
* `tqdm` for progress bars, `argparse` for CLI arguments
* `@property` only for trivial field access, never for computation or IO
content.rawContent:
---
paths:
  - "**/*.py"
---

# Python Style (Critical Rules)

Full guide: `arf/styleguide/python_styleguide.md`

* Python 3.12+ syntax (`list[int]` not `List[int]`, `X | None` not `Optional[X]`)
* Line length: 100 characters
* Absolute imports only, never relative
* Keyword arguments for 2+ heterogeneous parameters
* `@dataclass(frozen=True, slots=True)` for all structured data; never return tuples
* Centralize all file paths as `Path` constants in `paths.py`
* Named constants for all magic strings; no hardcoded values
* Strict mypy with explicit type annotations everywhere
* Explicit checks: `if x is None:` not `if not x:`, `if len(lst) == 0:` not `if not lst:`
* `pathlib.Path` always, never `os.path`
* `tqdm` for progress bars, `argparse` for CLI arguments
* `@property` only for trivial field access, never for computation or IO

content.content:
# Task Document Rules

## research.md sections

Objective, Background, Methodology Review, Key Findings, Recommended Approach, References.
Full spec: `arf/specifications/research_papers_specification.md`

## plan.md sections

Objective, Approach, Cost Estimation, Step by Step, Remote Machines, Assets Needed,
Expected Assets, Time Estimation, Risks & Fallbacks, Verification Criteria.

## results.md sections

Summary (2-3 sentences with headline metrics), Methodology (machine, runtime, timestamps),
Metrics Tables (per-category + aggregate + stddev), Comparison vs Baselines (always deltas),
Visualizations (embed charts via `![desc](images/file.png)`), Analysis/Discussion, Limitations, Verification,
Files Created, Next Steps/Suggestions.

## Results standards

* Use consistent metrics across tasks for cross-task comparison
* Always include baselines; report improvements as deltas
* Document negative results with the same rigor as positive results
* Include machine specs, runtime, timestamps, worker count for reproducibility
* All charts/graphs saved to `results/images/` and **embedded** in
  `results_detailed.md` with `![description](images/filename.png)` syntax
  so they render visually on GitHub — never just list filenames as text

## Code organization per task

* `paths.py` for centralized `pathlib.Path` constants
* `constants.py` for column names, dtypes, magic strings as typed constants
content.rawContent:
---
paths:
  - "tasks/**"
---

## results.md sections

## Results standards

* Use consistent metrics across tasks for cross-task comparison
* Always include baselines; report improvements as deltas

* All charts/graphs saved to `results/images/` and **embedded** in
